title: Shattering Brazilian Melons Paella
description: A savory, show-off Brazilian dish featuring juicy melons and crispy, score-fat components.

ingredients.name: Brazilian melon
ingredients.notes: Use any variety of sweet melon

ingredients.name: Spanish chorizo
ingredients.notes: Choose a mild or spicy type

instructions: Cook the rice according to package instructions.
Heat the olive oil in a large paella pan over medium-high heat.
Sauté the sliced chorizo until browned, about 5 minutes.
Remove the chorizo from the pan and set aside.
Sauté the diced onion until caramelized, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
Add the cubed melon and cook until lightly browned, about 3 minutes.
Add the ancho chili powder and saffron, and stir to combine.
Add the cooked rice, chicken broth, and toasted squash seeds to the pan.
Stir to combine and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
Remove the lid and stir in the cooked chorizo.
Return the paella to the oven and cook, uncovered, at 400°F (200°C) for 10-15 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned and crunchy.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
